reasons.1.dxc6 lines > respond to dxc6: Double check from Queen and Bishop

reasons.1.cxb5 lines > respond to Na3: Kc2 will lead to Kxc2 which will guard the Queen

reasons.1.c4 lines: Better development than white. Both Bishops are open, Queen has open line
To avoid the week backward pawn, white will play this move mostly.

reasons.1.Bd2 lines > respond to Bd2: Don't exchange an actively developed piece for a backward piece
Double attack on King and Bishop
Discover check to King by Queen!

reasons.1.c6 lines > c6 variation: White cannot directly push d4 as both Knight and Queen are attacking that square. So, they'll add a defender for d4.
Too add pressure to e pawn and put pressure to King's file
Otherwise Kc2 will for King and Queen

reasons.1.Nc3 lines > respond to Nc3: Because in the last line e5->e4 cause a lot of trouble for white. Kc3 guards e3
Trying to weaken the King's side. Also, attacks the hanging black Bishop
Add another attacker to d4 by saving King

reasons.1.Kd1 lines > respond to Kd1: dxc6 is not possible as pawn is pinned with King.

reasons.1.Kd1 lines > respond to Nxc6: c6 Knight is pinned with Queen. Bc2+ fork is on the next move
